prompt:
Add two more nix-daemon resource-yielding knobs to PR #515. These are NixOS-native options purpose-built for the exact problem this PR addresses (heavy nix builds competing with an interactive desktop session for CPU/disk).

## Change to make
In `modules/common/system.nix`, alongside the existing `nix.settings` block (or wherever fits the file's structure best), add:

```nix
# Run nix-daemon (and its build children) at SCHED_IDLE / IO class "idle"
# so they only get CPU and disk bandwidth when nothing interactive wants
# them. Complements the memory caps above: those bound how much; these
# bound when. Together they make the nightly auto-upgrade essentially
# invisible to a live desktop session.
nix.daemonCPUSchedPolicy = lib.mkDefault "idle";
nix.daemonIOSchedClass   = lib.mkDefault "idle";
```

Use `lib.mkDefault` for both, same reasoning as the existing changes — `klaus-worker-0` (microVM build worker) shouldn't yield to anything because there's no interactive workload to yield to.

## Why this is safe
- These set systemd `CPUSchedulingPolicy=idle` and `IOSchedulingClass=idle` on `nix-daemon.service`; the daemon's build subprocesses inherit.
- SCHED_IDLE tasks only run when no other runnable task wants the CPU. On an idle box, nix-daemon gets the whole CPU. On a busy box, it yields. This is exactly the desired behavior for a nightly maintenance task.
- "idle" IO class means daemon I/O only happens when no other process wants the disk. Eliminates the swap-in stall path on NVMe with mq-deadline.
- The only practical downside: an *interactive* `nix build` invocation will be slower if the user is simultaneously doing other heavy work. Acceptable — matches user preference ("I don't care how long the nightly build takes").

## CRITICAL: same no-local-build discipline as the rest of this PR
- No `nixos-rebuild`, no `nix build`, no `nix flake check`, no `nix eval`.
- CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) does dry-run builds on a runner; that's the validation gate.
- Verifying locally is what wedged the original agent on this very change.

## Steps
1. Read `modules/common/system.nix` to see the current state on the PR branch.
2. Add the two options with the comment block. Place them with the other `nix.` settings so the section reads coherently.
3. Read the file back to sanity-check syntax (matched braces, no typos).
4. Commit with a message like "system: yield nix-daemon to interactive workloads via SCHED_IDLE".
5. Push to the existing PR branch.
6. Update the PR description (`gh pr edit 515 --body ...`) to add a section describing these two new knobs and what they protect against, alongside the existing four mitigations. Don't lose the existing description content.

## Acceptance
- Two options added under `lib.mkDefault`
- Pushed to the existing PR branch (no new PR)
- PR description updated to cover all six mitigations now
- CI green (pipeline tracks this)
